title: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Data Localization and Cross-Border Transfer Field Validation

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.34: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Data Localization and Cross-Border Transfer Field Validation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.34' running on dora_outsourcing_register.csv (contract_id, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism, data_categories), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup dora_outsourcing_register.csv
| where isnull(processing_regions) OR processing_regions="" OR isnull(transfer_mechanism) OR transfer_mechanism=""
| lookup restricted_data_categories.csv data_categories OUTPUT requires_scc
| where requires_scc="true" AND transfer_mechanism!="SCC"
| table contract_id, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism, data_categories
description: Outsourcing often imposes data residency constraints. Validating that register rows specify processing locations and transfer mechanisms reduces the risk of unlawful transfers and incomplete RoI disclosures.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Legal maintains `restricted_data_categories.csv`; (2) do not store SCC text in Splunk—boolean flags only; (3) alert privacy office; (4) sync with DPIA references; (5) annual RoI sign-off pack.
visualization: Table (defects), Map (optional region codes), Single value (count). Key fields: `contract_id`, `data_categories`, `processing_regions`, `transfer_mechanism`.
knownFalsePositives: Register changes during onboarding, contract renewals, or planned vendor consolidation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_outsourcing_register.csv` (contract_id, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism, data_categories).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Legal maintains `restricted_data_categories.csv`; (2) do not store SCC text in Splunk—boolean flags only; (3) alert privacy office; (4) sync with DPIA references; (5) annual RoI sign-off pack.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_outsourcing_register.csv
| where isnull(processing_regions) OR processing_regions="" OR isnull(transfer_mechanism) OR transfer_mechanism=""
| lookup restricted_data_categories.csv data_categories OUTPUT requires_scc
| where requires_scc="true" AND transfer_mechanism!="SCC"
| table contract_id, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism, data_categories
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Outsourcing Registers — Data Localization and Cross-Border Transfer Field Validation** — Outsourcing often imposes data residency constraints. Validating that register rows specify processing locations and transfer mechanisms reduces the risk of unlawful transfers and incomplete RoI disclosures.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_outsourcing_register.csv` (contract_id, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism, data_categories). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(processing_regions) OR processing_regions="" OR isnull(transfer_mechanism) OR transfer_mechanism=""` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where requires_scc="true" AND transfer_mechanism!="SCC"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: contract_id, data_categories, processing_regions, transfer_mechanism.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (defects), Map (optional region codes), Single value (count).
